methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Oklo
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $10B+

data.narrative:
Nano Nuclear Energy is a publicly traded nuclear energy company currently valued at $1.5B+, having raised over $600M to date. The valuation assessment draws from a comparable universe of 11 peers across the nuclear energy sector—3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private—representing companies at late-stage and public market stages with similar valuation tiers.

The peer-median method was applied using four directly comparable companies: TerraPower ($4.0B), X-Energy ($2.0B), Kairos Power ($1.6B), and Zap Energy ($1.0B). The median valuation across this cohort is $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.0B. This peer-median approach anchors the valuation to established same-sector benchmarks at comparable development stages, reducing reliance on single-point estimates.

The triangulated analysis yields a valuation range of $1.5B (low) to $2.1B (high), with a median of $1.8B. The current $1.5B valuation aligns with the lower bound, while the peer median suggests modest upside to $1.8B. Key uncertainties include regulatory pathway clarity, commercialization timeline execution, and relative positioning within the advanced nuclear cohort. The limited methodological convergence (single method applied) and spread across the peer set ($1.0B to $4.0B) warrant continued monitoring of stage progression and capital deployment efficiency.
